title: Skipping with 'continue'
description: How to skip an iteration safely.

slides.title: Skipping a Beat 🦘
slides.content:
## Don't Stop, Just Skip

We know `break` completely kills the loop.

But what if you just want to skip the *current* cycle and move on to the next one? 
*   *"If the file is empty, skip to the next file."*
*   *"If the number is odd, skip it."*

For this, we use the **`continue`** keyword.

slides.title: Evens Only! 🔢
slides.content: Let's print only the even numbers from 1 to 5.

quizData.question: What is the difference between `break` and `continue`?

options.text: They do the exact same thing.
continue skips the current iteration; break exits the loop entirely.
break skips the current iteration; continue exits the loop entirely.

quizData.explanation: `continue` jumps straight back to the top of the loop to evaluate the next cycle, effectively "skipping" the lines below it.

quizData.question: If `continue` is executed, the `post` statement (like `i++`) is also skipped.
quizData.explanation: False. `continue` skips the rest of the *code block*, but correctly executes the `post` statement (`i++`) so the loop doesn't get stuck forever!
